results.title: Overlap Between Categories for Data Center and Technology Business Emerges at County Planning Commission Meeting (6-11-2026)
results.description: Could a proposed hyperscale facility be intended to do things that are already described as by-right uses in Warren County’s ordinance? A public presentation at a County Planning Commission meeting from Kevin Cuddeback, Front Royal citizen and a member of the Town’s Board of Architectural Review, inspired the Royal Examiner to take a closer look at Section 180-28(B)(18) of the code. Continue reading Overlap Between Categories for Data Center and Technology Business Emerges at County Planning Commission Meeting at Royal Examiner.

results.title: 1965 Ford Falcon Squire Wagon 289
results.description: This 1965 Ford Falcon Squire wagon was initially built with a C-code 289ci V8, a C-4 automatic transmission, Raven Black paintwork, and a color-coordinated interior. The car is believed to have been originally sold in Connecticut, and it was stored for ~45 years before it was acquired by the seller in 2025. The seller subsequently rebuilt the brakes, suspension, and fuel system, mounted BFGoodrich Silvertown tires, had a custom dual exhaust system fabricated, and retrofitted an aluminum radiator and a PerTronix ignition system. The carpeting was also replaced and fluid changes were performed, and LED bulbs were also added for the headlights. This Falcon Squire is now offered in Massachusetts with the owner's manual, service records, a car cover, and a clean Montana title.
